Afrigen Kenya Scholars Analysis Notebook

Bonface Onyango

2026-04-06

Setup and Libraries

Activate r_env

  • Libraries installed in virtual r_env
  • conda activate r_env
# Core libraries 
library(dplyr) 
library(ggplot2) 
library(igraph) 
library(ggraph) 
library(readr)
library(devtools)

Intall lisearch

#uncomment or install in r env
#library(devtools)      # now should work
#install_github("elizagrames/litsearchr", ref="main")
library(litsearchr)
packageVersion("litsearchr")
[1] ‘1.0.0’

Pubmed search page

  • https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/advanced/

  • search terms (medication) AND (CBT) AND (phobia)

  • Query: ((medication) AND (CBT)) AND (phobia)

df_pubmed<-import_results(file="../pubmed-breastcanc-set.nbib")
Reading file ../pubmed-breastcanc-set.nbib ... done
df_pubmed <- as.data.frame(df_pubmed)
head(df_pubmed)

sum(sapply(df_pubmed$keywords, is.null))
[1] 193

Extract keywords->tagged

extract_terms(keywords=df_pubmed[, "keywords"], method="tagged")

Extract keywords

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)

clean_keywords <- function(x) {
  # Handle NULL or empty
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) return(character(0))
  
  # Remove NA
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(character(0))
  
  # Clean and split
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("\\n", " ") %>%     # remove newlines
    str_replace_all("\"", "") %>%       # remove quotes
    str_trim() %>%                      # trim whitespace
    str_split(",") %>%                  # split by comma
    unlist() %>%                        # flatten
    str_trim() %>%                      # trim again
    discard(~ .x %in% c("", "NULL", "character0"))
}

# Apply to df_pubmed$keywords and flatten
df_pubmed <- df_pubmed %>%
  mutate(keywords_clean = map(keywords, clean_keywords))

# Flatten all keywords
keywords_flat <- df_pubmed$keywords_clean %>%
  flatten_chr()

# Remove empty
keywords_flat <- keywords_flat[keywords_flat != ""]
keywords_flat[1:5]
[1] "BRCA1"         "BRCA2"         "breast cancer" "ethnicity"     "geography"    
# Preview

Tagged keywords

keywords_extracted <- extract_terms(
  keywords = keywords_flat,
  method = "tagged"
)

# --- Step 4: Preview ---
keywords_extracted[1:20]
 [1] "adjuvant therapy"         "advanced breast cancer"   "african ancestry"        
 [4] "breast cancer"            "breast self-examination"  "breast tumours"          
 [7] "cancer genetics"          "cell proliferation"       "cervical cancer"         
[10] "chk2 inhibitors"          "colorectal cancer"        "coping strategies"       
[13] "developing countries"     "ductal carcinoma in situ" "early detection"         
[16] "economic evaluation"      "extracellular matrix"     "female thorax"           
[19] "financial toxicity"       "gene expression"

Tagged keywords -> min1

keywords_extracted_min_1 <- extract_terms(
  keywords = keywords_flat,
  method = "tagged",
  min_n = 1
)

min_n=2)

title_terms <- extract_terms(
text=df_pubmed[, "title"],
method="fakerake",
min_freq=3, min_n=2,
stopwords=all_stopwords
)

[73] "sub-saharan africa"                             
[74] "susceptibility locus"                           
[75] "triple-negative breast"                         
[76] "triple-negative breast cancer"                  
[77] "years lived"                                    
[78] "young women"

Keywords + title terms

terms <- unique(c(keywords_extracted_min_1, title_terms))

docs <- paste(df_pubmed[, "title"], df_pubmed[, "abstract"])
docs[1]
[1] "Global patterns and trends in breast cancer incidence and mortality across 185 countries. Updates of current and projected estimates of the burden are critical to monitoring the success of ongoing efforts in breast cancer control, such as the World Health Organization Global Breast Cancer Initiative, which aims to reduce breast cancer mortality by 2.5% per year. We investigated the current (2022) and future (2050) global burden of female breast cancer overall, and by age group, in 185 countries using the GLOBOCAN database, and 10-year trends in incidence and mortality rates in 50 and 46 countries, respectively, using the Cancer Incidence in Five Continents plus and World Health Organization mortality databases. Globally, 2.3 million new cases and 670,000 deaths from female breast cancer occurred in 2022. Annual rates increased by 1-5% in half of examined countries. Mortality rates decreased in 29 countries with very high Human Development Index (HDI), and seven countries (for example, Belgium and Denmark) are meeting the Global Breast Cancer Initiative goal of at least a 2.5% decrease each year. By 2050, new cases and deaths will have increased by 38% and 68%, respectively, disproportionately impacting low-HDI countries. High-quality cancer and vital status data, and continued progress in early diagnosis and access to treatment, are needed in countries with low and medium HDI to address inequities and monitor cancer control goals."
dfm <- create_dfm(elements=docs, features=terms)
dfm[1:5, 1:7]
     3-f adjuvant therapy advanced breast cancer africa african african ancestry apoptosis
[1,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
[2,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
[3,]   0                0                      0      1       1                0         0
[4,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         1
[5,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
g <- create_network(dfm, min_studies=3)
ggraph(g, layout="stress") +
coord_fixed() +
expand_limits(x=c(-3, 3)) +
geom_edge_link(aes(alpha=weight)) +
geom_node_point(shape="circle filled", fill="white") +
geom_node_text(aes(label=name), hjust="outward", check_overlap=TRUE) +
guides(edge_alpha=FALSE)

strengths <- strength(g)
data.frame(term=names(strengths), strength=strengths, row.names=NULL) %>%
mutate(rank=rank(strength, ties.method="min")) %>%
arrange(strength) ->
term_strengths
term_strengths
cutoff_fig <- ggplot(term_strengths, aes(x=rank, y=strength, label=term)) +
geom_line() +
geom_point() +
geom_text(data=filter(term_strengths, rank>5), hjust="right", nudge_y=20,
check_overlap=TRUE)
cutoff_fig

cutoff_cum <- find_cutoff(g, method="cumulative", percent=0.8)
cutoff_cum
[1] 181
cutoff_fig +
geom_hline(yintercept=cutoff_cum, linetype="dashed")

get_keywords(reduce_graph(g, cutoff_cum))
 [1] "africa"                        "african"                      
 [3] "african ancestry"              "biomarker"                    
 [5] "brca"                          "brca1"                        
 [7] "brca2"                         "breast"

cutoff_change <- find_cutoff(g, method="changepoint", knot_num=3)
cutoff_change
[1]  387  855 1378 3447
cutoff_fig +
geom_hline(yintercept=cutoff_change, linetype="dashed")

g_redux <- reduce_graph(g, cutoff_change[1])
selected_terms <- get_keywords(g_redux)
selected_terms
 [1] "africa"                 "african"                "brca"                  
 [4] "brca1"                  "brca2"                  "breast"

"medication",
"cognitive-behavioural therapy",
"cognitive behavioural therapy"
)

Optional: GitHub installs

library(devtools) # install_github(“elizagrames/litsearchr”, ref=“main”) library(litsearchr)

Ensure plots display in notebook

options(jupyter.plot_mimetypes = c(“image/png”, “image/svg+xml”))
